options.expected_information_value: potentially high as a structurally different scheduled-event state
options.omission_reason: no local historical calendar source with exact event-time vintage and runtime provenance is currently registered
options.opportunity_cost: open another source program before disposing the completed VIX audit

options.expected_information_value: highest because the exact source audit already proves historical roll and adjustment semantics are not identifiable
options.opportunity_cost: forego VIX performance use until exact point-in-time provenance exists

payload.rationale: Prune the FPMarkets VIX source axis because 196027 structurally valid M5 rows still lack a historical constituent contract map, roll schedule, and adjustment methodology. The source remains context-only and cannot receive a SourcePermit or performance interpretation.

axes.causal_question: Can causal complementary sleeves and exposure netting reduce time-side concentration beyond a single directional sleeve without changing signals?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two complementary constructions fail concentration and net economics; reopen only with an independently supported sleeve.
axes.why_now: Positive legacy totals repeatedly failed concentration gates under a single-sleeve architecture.

axes.causal_question: Does causal spread-aware entry deferral or abstention preserve gross signal utility beyond unconditional next-open execution under identical entries?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two causal execution policies fail gross-to-net recovery; reopen only with a materially different executable quote state.
axes.why_now: Every performance Study modeled costs but none directly isolated causal execution timing as the primary layer.

axes.causal_question: Does a fold-trained cost-aware utility objective improve a fixed model and label beyond an unweighted directional objective?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two bounded utility objectives fail their unweighted controls; reopen only with a new causal cost observable.
axes.why_now: The fixed-lot target is economic but prior fitted models did not directly contrast a native-cost utility objective.

axes.causal_question: Can a 12-bar terminal label, 70th-percentile selector, and 12-bar lifecycle create a profitable one-plus-entry-per-day long sleeve beyond the sparse volatility-clock 48-bar control?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the sparse-48 versus dense-12 structural extreme; reopen only with a distinct short-horizon event mechanism, new information, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: The best current sleeve is profitable but produces only 0.176 entries per day and fails monthly drawdown concentration, far from the final five-to-ten daily composite target.

axes.causal_question: Does a long-only equity-premium direction gate improve stability and utility beyond symmetric long-short entry under the same volatility-clock signal?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the symmetric-versus-long-only extreme; reopen only with a distinct causal short-risk state, external macro state, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: The volatility-clock surface earned 2330520000 of 2349550000 micropoints on long entries, making symmetric direction a material untested assumption.

axes.causal_question: Does a causal first-passage path label identify stable directional utility beyond a matched terminal-return label with the signal chassis fixed?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two distinct path-label contrasts show no separation; reopen only with a distinct event horizon or untouched material.
axes.why_now: Prior feature and analog Studies repeatedly used fixed forward-return semantics and leave target mismatch untested.

axes.causal_question: Does a fold-trained pairwise-interaction ridge model identify stable utility beyond a linear ridge control with inputs and trading fixed?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two distinct fitted-capacity contrasts fail; reopen only with validator-demonstrated residual structure or untouched material.
axes.why_now: Current-Mission target Studies fixed linear ridge capacity, while prior nonlinear work changed hand-built feature scores rather than fitted capacity.

axes.causal_question: Can a fold-train native-utility selector choose high-volatility target follow or reverse causally and stabilize the activity-reaching composite beyond fixed reversal?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact fold-train follow-versus-reverse role selector; do not add role candidates, training windows, metrics, thresholds, or fallback variants.
axes.why_now: STU-0092 reached 5.143 entries per day, 1.194 PF, and strong stress utility but fixed reversal lost the first three folds.

axes.causal_question: Does train-only upper-tercile volatility routing preserve the dense 12-bar sleeve's high-volatility profit while removing low- and middle-volatility losses?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the unconditional-dense versus train-upper-tercile-volatility extreme; reopen only with a distinct point-in-time state, new information, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0079 directly localized all dense-sleeve profit to the high-volatility regime while low and middle regimes were negative.

axes.causal_question: Does a causal completed-bar 192-bar positive-trend gate reduce drawdown concentration while preserving the long-only volatility-clock signal?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the unconditional-long versus positive-192-bar-trend extreme; reopen only with a materially distinct causal macro state or untouched material.
axes.why_now: The long-only Study was positive across eight folds and all volatility regimes but failed monthly drawdown concentration, identifying market state rather than direction as the next bottleneck.

axes.causal_question: Does abstaining from every fold-train low-volatility entry isolate a higher-quality fixed-lot core from the activity-reaching session-dense portfolio?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact all-regime control versus full low-volatility abstention ablation; do not tune volatility cutoffs or remove only selected sides or sleeves.
axes.why_now: STU-0090 attributed only 12430000 micropoints to 1456 low-volatility trades while middle and high regimes carried nearly all utility.

axes.causal_question: Does a fixed causal fold-train US100-US500 market residual portfolio create stable six-bar mean-reversion utility beyond a source-bound target-only control, with continuation as a fixed direction contrast?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact registered baseline, residual mean-reversion primary, and residual continuation direction contrast; reopen only with independent confirmation, untouched material, or genuinely different benchmark semantics.
axes.why_now: The sign-coherence router had no information, while the exact source and registered target-only baseline now support a validator-proven systemic-removal portfolio contrast with two unexposed residual subjects.

axes.causal_question: Does a causal fold-train US100-US500 market residual event create stable six-bar mean-reversion or continuation utility beyond a source-bound target-only control after native and stressed costs?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact target-only mean-reversion, residual mean-reversion, and residual continuation comparison; reopen only with independent confirmation, untouched material, or genuinely different benchmark semantics, never beta lookback selector or holding grids.
axes.why_now: The exact US500 sign-coherence router had no incremental information, while the qualified source permits a materially different systemic-versus-idiosyncratic decomposition architecture and the current frontier still fails early-fold and monthly concentration.

axes.causal_question: Does a causal monthly break-even entry lock reduce realized drawdown concentration while preserving the dense regime-direction router's fixed-lot economics?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the unrestricted-router versus causal monthly break-even lock extreme; reopen only with a materially different point-in-time risk budget, independent sleeve netting, confirmation material after passing discovery, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0081 reached 2.276 entries per day and 6317550000 micropoints but failed the monthly realized drawdown share at 1396291 ppm.

axes.causal_question: Can two independently positive fixed-lot US100 sleeves increase native and stressed portfolio utility while reducing loss concentration versus the regime router alone?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact router-only versus dual-positive fixed-lot composition; reopen only with a causal portfolio loss-timing response, independent confirmation, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0086 target-only US100 direction and STU-0081 regime router are both positive under native and stressed costs, unlike the failed target-downside sleeve.

axes.causal_question: Does fold-only probability calibration with abstention convert a fixed ranking score into stable net utility beyond a percentile selector?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two calibration families fail ranking-to-utility conversion; reopen only with independent calibration material.
axes.why_now: Several legacy Studies had positive aggregate KPI but failed stability and concentration while calibration had no direct typed coverage.

axes.causal_question: Does high-volatility long momentum plus low- and middle-volatility inverse routing recover dense activity while reducing monthly realized drawdown versus high-volatility-only long routing?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the high-volatility-long-only versus high-long-plus-calm-inverse extreme; reopen only with a distinct causal routing state, new directional information, independent confirmation after passing discovery, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0079 showed dense activity but calm-regime losses, while STU-0080 showed profitable high-volatility momentum but sparse activity and unresolved monthly drawdown concentration.

axes.causal_question: Does the fixed broker-15-22 session gate make a 90th-percentile six-bar US100 direction sleeve reach composite activity with positive stressed utility beyond the router control?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact router-control versus session-dense subject extreme; do not tune adjacent quantiles or hour windows.
axes.why_now: The 90th-percentile target sleeve is positive only in broker hours 15 through 22 and this exact gate reaches 5.1 entries per day with positive stress utility.

axes.causal_question: Does reserving a stopped position slot through its original maximum expiry preserve volatility-stop loss control without destructive immediate re-entry?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the immediate-reuse versus original-expiry shadow-reservation extreme; reopen only with a distinct causal position-state gate or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0075 showed that early stop exits released scarce slots, doubled trade count, and destroyed utility, identifying lifecycle reuse as the mechanism.

axes.causal_question: Does abstaining only in the losing low-volatility state preserve middle-volatility inverse and high-volatility long utility while reducing monthly drawdown concentration?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the two-way calm-inverse versus low-abstain three-way extreme; do not reopen without new causal state information, independent confirmation after passing discovery, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: STU-0081 directly measured low-volatility inverse loss, middle-volatility inverse gain, and high-volatility long gain with 2.276 entries per day; this new family preserves that prior exposure rather than resetting it.

axes.causal_question: Does causal completed-bar US30 downside-volatility expansion lead short-horizon US100 direction beyond symmetric-source and target-only controls after native costs?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two distinct asymmetric US30 shock Studies fail incremental information; reopen only with a changed eligible source, independent confirmation after passing discovery, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: Qualified US30 cash-index M5 permits an asymmetric blue-chip downside-leadership test after symmetric relative strength failed every fold and regime.

axes.causal_question: Does causal completed-bar US30 versus US100 sector rotation add incremental US100 directional information beyond target-only controls after native costs?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after source qualification plus two distinct sector-rotation Studies fail incremental information; reopen only with a changed eligible source contract, independent confirmation after passing discovery, or untouched material.
axes.why_now: US30 cash-index M5 has exact local FPMarkets clock coverage; endogenous router, hard risk lock, and pairwise model changes failed the final concentration endpoint.

axes.causal_question: Can FPMarkets US500 M5 satisfy exact historical and local runtime eligibility as a point-in-time market-beta source for later US100 residual-state research?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after exact historical audit and runtime availability proof; no performance read is allowed until the source becomes runtime eligible.
axes.why_now: The local FPMarkets terminal exposes US500 from 2018-05-01 through the development boundary with exact M5 fields, while VIX begins only in 2020.

axes.causal_question: Can fixed causal US500-US100 sign coherence route only the high-volatility target role and reduce STU-0092 stability concentration without changing its underlying sleeves or activity?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after the exact fixed sign-coherence router versus STU-0092 fixed reversal; reopen only with independent material or genuinely different benchmark semantics, never adjacent lookbacks thresholds or role names.
axes.why_now: STU-0092 reached 5.143 entries per day and strong native and stressed profit but retained early-fold and monthly drawdown concentration; US500 now supplies a distinct broad-market state.

axes.causal_question: Does the exact FPMarkets VIX M5 history provide a causal point-in-time risk-aversion state aligned to completed US100 bars across the observed development prefix with a fresh runtime receipt?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop if timestamp coverage completed-bar alignment schema freshness or runtime retrieval fails; after qualification preserve the source and allow only one fixed VIX-state performance mechanism.
axes.why_now: The price-direction and market-residual families are exhausted while the live FPMarkets environment exposes VIX M5 as a materially different option-implied risk state.

axes.causal_question: Does a volatility-clock terminal label identify stable utility beyond a fixed 48-bar first-passage control with trading fixed?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after two distinct activity-time budgets fail; reopen only with point-in-time macro state or untouched material.
axes.why_now: Fixed-clock labels assume equal information speed across quiet, open, and event markets; a variance budget tests activity time directly.

axes.causal_question: Does a pre-entry volatility loss stop reduce realized drawdown concentration without destroying the volatility-clock signal utility?
axes.stop_or_reopen_condition: Stop after one no-stop versus pre-entry variance-stop extreme and one distinct risk state fail; reopen only with point-in-time regime state or untouched material.
axes.why_now: The volatility-clock label was stress-positive across eight folds but failed the monthly realized drawdown share, directly identifying a risk bottleneck.

exhaustion_standard.stop_basis: Each axis needs two distinct negative Studies and Executables with all six evidence modes; the terminal also needs five layers, five architectures, and no unresolved candidate-eligible positive.

payload.opportunity_cost_basis: Prune the unidentifiable VIX rolling source before any performance value; reopen only with exact point-in-time roll and adjustment provenance.

bounds.activity_policy: no abstention, no added gate, and exact baseline event timestamps and sleeve assignment
bounds.baseline: reuse the exact registered STU-0092 high-volatility target-reversal executable
bounds.confirmation: no candidate claim without independent untouched confirmation
bounds.development_access: registered development material only; no quarantine, holdout, or live values
bounds.execution: native causal spread plus registered stress, fixed next-open execution, and one lot per sleeve
bounds.horizons: router 12 bars and target sleeve 6 bars fixed
bounds.model_surface: one deterministic depth-bounded nonlinear classifier with no hyperparameter grid
bounds.selector_policy: reuse exact baseline selectors, volatility cutoffs, and broker-session window

done_conditions: register one canonical synthesis architecture and immutable axis before performance access
compare the one new executable against the exact reused STU-0092 baseline
prove event timestamp, sleeve, count, size, execution, and hold invariance
report native and stress economics, activity, fold stability, regime concentration, and monthly realized-exit drawdown share
prune on absent separation or retain only through the typed confirmation path
objective.objective: Test whether one fold-trained shallow nonlinear event-level direction meta-policy improves the exact STU-0092 activity-reaching portfolio without changing its event schedule, fixed one-lot sizing, or sleeve holding periods.

options.expected_information_value: high: one exact per-event joint-state direction contrast tests missing conditional information while holding activity and lifecycle fixed
options.opportunity_cost: one new Portfolio axis followed by at most one registered Batch and one trial

options.expected_information_value: moderate: a rates source may add macro state but requires source eligibility before performance evidence
options.omission_reason: the current Initiative already binds one exact label-model-trade-synthesis contrast over STU-0092 and no performance value has been observed
options.opportunity_cost: defer one source audit until the exact native-state synthesis contrast is resolved

payload.rationale: Admit a genuinely distinct joint-sleeve event-direction synthesis mechanism: every existing STU-0092 event receives one fold-trained follow-or-reverse action, with no abstention, event, hold, lot, selector, session, cost, or execution change. This is not the failed fold-wide role selection axis and it receives only one fixed shallow-tree contrast.

payload.rationale: Execute exactly one shallow per-event direction synthesis contrast on the newly admitted axis. The exact STU-0092 event schedule, selectors, sessions, native cost, one-lot sizing, and 12/6-bar holds remain controlled; every event must receive follow or reverse and no hyperparameter search is allowed.

one depth-three minimum-leaf-128 tree over existing joint event state
mandatory per-event follow-or-reverse trade and synthesis action for both sleeves

same US100 observed development material and nine rolling folds
same existing five path features router score target score volatility tercile and slot identity
same train-only router and target selector thresholds and volatility cutoffs
same broker 15-to-22 target session and underlying two sleeve event schedule
same next-open execution one lot per slot native and stressed costs and 12-or-6-bar holds
every existing event receives follow or reverse with no zero action or abstention
fixed sklearn 1.8.0 log-loss tree without depth leaf feature threshold or fallback grid
global selection exposure total 593
no holdout candidate runtime or live authority
done_conditions: reuse the exact STU-0092 control and register exactly one new subject Executable
require zero intent schedule executed schedule event count hold and abstention mismatches
derive the subject verdict from durable registered-validator evidence
preserve any rejection as exact negative memory
